summary: R2 fixes satisfy the prior systems_architect blockers. The branch diff remains inside allowed paths, M4 save runtime state now uses explicit nested DTO/parser/copy paths that strip unknown nested keys before hydration and reject malformed nested fields, and M5 worker-compatible evidence now crosses a real Node worker_threads JSON message boundary inside allowed apps/sim-runner/test paths. No forbidden UI/client/content/manual battle/telemetry/server/multiplayer/mod scope was introduced.

decisions: ACCEPT R2. The previous P1 save-format blocker is resolved: packages/save-format/src/index.ts now declares explicit nested M4 save DTOs, parses each M4 collection and nested union/object field, and reconstructs only known fields through copyM4CampaignState and nested copy functions. Unknown nested keys are stripped before hydration; malformed nested values are rejected with bounded invalid-schema reasons.

ACCEPT R2 worker evidence. apps/sim-runner/src/m5-playable-loop-worker.mjs uses node:worker_threads and JSON string request/response boundaries; run-determinism and tests/sim-core-m5-playable-loop.test.ts both exercise that boundary. This is sufficient for the task's Worker-host contract evidence within allowed paths because apps/web/client paths are explicitly forbidden for this task.

The M5 playable loop remains grounded in authoritative sim-core state and versioned GameCommandV1 paths. The success path reaches accepted M4 campaign/logistics/automatic engagement/siege/withdrawal/postwar handoff surfaces; the duplicate postwar command failure path preserves the state hash.

Single-writer WorldState authority, deterministic stable IDs, stable ordering/hash evidence, and bounded serializable errors remain acceptable. sim-core does not import React, Pixi, Electron, DOM, Node filesystem, or real-time APIs.

The new sim.m3-postwar-governance-applied event-tail parser is complete enough for this slice: it explicitly validates schemaVersion, actor, settlementId, method, polity/district IDs, obligationIds, administrativeLoad, reasonCodes, and revisions, and rejects bad data through invalid-schema paths.

No ADR or Human Gate is required for this R2 acceptance. The change is an explicit schema evolution and worker-evidence improvement inside allowed implementation/test paths, not a frozen architecture change, irreversible migration, or formal M5 milestone passage.

risks.summary: The worker-compatible proof is a Node worker_threads boundary, not a browser Worker. This is acceptable for this task because apps/web/client changes are forbidden; a future web-client integration task should add browser Worker coverage in its own allowed scope.
packages/protocol has no package-level typecheck script. This is pre-existing/non-blocking for this review because protocol tests passed and root pnpm test passed.

requested_action: Proceed with QA review of M5-SIM-PLAYABLE-LOOP-001 R2 on feat/m5-sim-playable-loop. Focus QA on the accepted M5 start-to-success/failure loop, save/load persistence of M4 runtime state, malformed save/event rejection, determinism evidence, and scope boundaries.
